name: greenlet (Micro-threads for Python)

performance.notes: Extremely lightweight; microsecond context switching vs millisecond threads

agent_readiness.notes: Low-level micro-thread (coroutine) primitive for Python. from greenlet import greenlet. def task1(): print('t1'); gr2.switch(); print('t1 again'). def task2(): print('t2'); gr1.switch(). gr1 = greenlet(task1); gr2 = greenlet(task2); gr1.switch(). Explicit cooperative scheduling via .switch(). Used as foundation for gevent. gr.dead property. gr.parent for parent greenlet. greenlet.getcurrent(). Pass values: gr.switch(value) / value = othergr.switch(). Throw exceptions: gr.throw(ExceptionType). C extension — very fast switching. Primary use: as a building block for higher-level frameworks (gevent, eventlet, SQLAlchemy greenlet integration).

gotchas: Very low-level — most users should use gevent or asyncio instead
No IO integration — just coroutine switching
Not compatible with Python async/await directly
best_when: When you need ultra-low-overhead cooperative scheduling primitives
avoid_when: Application-level concurrency — use asyncio, trio, or gevent
New projects needing concurrency (asyncio is the standard)
When you don't need to build a concurrency framework
summary: greenlet provides low-level coroutine (micro-thread) primitives for Python. greenlet(fn); gr.switch() for cooperative scheduling. C extension for microsecond switching. MIT. Foundation for gevent and SQLAlchemy async. Very low-level — most should use asyncio, trio, or gevent. Explicit switch() scheduling. No IO integration. Use only when building concurrency frameworks or working with gevent internals.
